zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Gleason II於1572年11月23日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾列沃斯時，他的父親Thomas Gleason Jr.25歲，母親Jane Lowe20歲。他於1602年10月28日與Ann Armesby在英國英格蘭素福爾可考費爾德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1609年3月22日在英國素福爾可考費爾德享年36歲。

nameMeanings.name: Gleason
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Waterford): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Glasáin, from a diminutive of glas ‘green, blue, gray’.

variants.name: Glasheen
meanings: Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Glaisín,based on a diminutive of glas ‘green, blue, gray’. Compare Gleason .

variants.name: Glisan
meanings: Possibly an altered form of Irish   Gleason  . This surname is concentrated in IL and PA.

variants.name: Gleeson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Donal, Siobhan, Brendan, Conor.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Gleason II was born on 23 November 1572, in Culworth, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Thomas Gleason Jr., was 25 and his mother, Jane Lowe, was 20. He married Ann Armesby on 28 October 1602, in Cockfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 22 March 1609, in Cockfield, Suffolk, England, at the age of 36.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Gleason II(이)가 1572년 11월 23일 영국 노르섬프토실르 컬워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Gleason Jr.(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Lowe(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1602년 10월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 콕필드에서 Ann Armesby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1609년 3월 22일 영국 수폴크 콕필드에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.
